data.title: ECB President Christine Lagarde Considers Early Departure Amid Digital Euro Advancements
data.description: Christine Lagarde, President of the European Central Bank (ECB), is reportedly contemplating an early departure from her role to potentially influence the selection of her successor. According to NS3.AI, this consideration comes as the ECB progresses the digital euro project into its subsequent development phase. These developments underscore notable leadership and regulatory shifts within the ECB, which could have implications for the future of digital currency in Europe.

data.title: Nakamoto completes acquisition of BTC Inc and UTXO for approximately $81.63 million.
data.description: Nakamoto, a Bitcoin treasury company, announced the completion of its acquisition of Bitcoin media and events company BTC Inc. and investment firm UTXO Management, making both companies wholly owned subsidiaries. The transaction was entirely paid for with Nakamoto common stock and options. According to the disclosure, holders of securities in BTC Inc. and UTXO received a total of 364,795,104 Nakamoto common shares on a fully diluted basis, valued at approximately $81.63 million. BTC Inc. is the organizer of The Bitcoin Conference and the parent company of Bitcoin Magazine.

data.title: Since its merger, Ethereum's circulating supply has increased by over 950,000 coins, with an annualized inflation rate of approximately 0.23%.
data.description: According to ultrasound.money data, Ethereum's circulating supply has increased by over 950,000 since the merge and upgrade. The current total circulating supply of Ethereum is approximately 121.5 million, with an annualized inflation rate of approximately 0.23%.

data.title: USDC Circulation Increases by 700 Million in One Week
data.description: Circle's website reveals that in the week ending February 19, USDC issuance reached approximately 5.3 billion, while redemptions totaled around 4.6 billion, resulting in a net increase of about 700 million USDC in circulation. According to Foresight News, as of February 19, the total circulation of USDC stood at approximately 73.7 billion, with reserve assets valued at around 74 billion dollars.

data.title: Ethereum Holds Steady at $1900, Indicating Weakening Seller Pressure
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X. Ethereum (ETH) is currently maintaining its position around the $1900 mark, suggesting a stabilization in its market activity. This development indicates that sellers are losing momentum, as the cryptocurrency has not continued its previous downward trend. While there is no immediate need for a price surge, maintaining this support level is considered a positive sign for Ethereum's market strength.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Markets Stable Despite New US Tariff Announcement
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced a new universal 10% tariff following a Supreme Court decision that limited his emergency powers. According to NS3.AI, despite this development, cryptocurrency markets remained stable, with Bitcoin and Ether prices showing resilience. The administration has shifted to other legal authorities for implementing the tariffs, moving away from the emergency powers that were blocked by the court. Meanwhile, the number of Bitcoin millionaire addresses has decreased over the past year, indicating that recent regulatory optimism has not yet resulted in sustained on-chain wealth growth.

data.title: Sonic Labs Launches AI-Powered Web3 Application Platform
data.description:
Sonic Labs has introduced Spawn, an AI-driven platform for building Web3 applications. According to ChainCatcher, Spawn enables the generation, compilation, and deployment of smart contracts using natural language. It also automatically creates front-end interfaces and wallet integration features.

The platform allows users to convert English descriptions into production-grade smart contracts, deploy them on-chain, and generate complete front-end interfaces. This process abstracts the full stack from contract logic to UI and on-chain interaction. Users can engage in conversational iterative development with an integrated AI assistant to modify contract logic, front-end components, and deployment configurations, ultimately deploying applications to the Sonic test network.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ether Hold Strong as Trump Announces Additional Universal 10% Tariff
data.description:
Cryptocurrency markets showed resilience Friday after US President Donald Trump unveiled a new universal 10% tariff on imports.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoin-ether-hold-strong-as-trump-announces-additional-universal-10-tariff/

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia-Japan Relations Hit Low Amid Hostilities
data.description: On February 20, Russian Presidential Press Secretary Dmitry Peskov stated that relations between Russia and Japan have reached a freezing point due to Japan's persistent hostility towards Russia. According to Jin10, Peskov emphasized that without a change in relations, achieving any peace agreement is impossible. As reported by the Russian News Agency, Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi remarked in a policy speech on February 20 that despite the severe state of Japan-Russia relations, Tokyo remains committed to resolving the 'territorial issue' and concluding a peace treaty with Russia.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold Market Sentiment Remains Bullish Despite Tariff Ruling
data.description: Gold prices experienced a sharp decline following a Supreme Court tariff ruling, yet bullish sentiment quickly regained dominance, according to Jin10. John Weyey, head of the commercial hedging department at Walsh Trading, noted that despite losing a potential positive factor, gold retains significant upward momentum. Many market participants continue to buy gold due to its ongoing rally, which has persisted over the past six months. Weyey believes that the market will maintain this strategy until a bearish signal emerges. Although the tariff decision has reduced some uncertainty, other risks remain, supporting the continued rise in gold prices.

data.title: Russia Warns of Security Risks from Telegram Use by Troops
data.description: Russia has raised concerns about the use of the Telegram app by its troops on the front line, suggesting that Ukraine could potentially access sensitive information through the platform. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increasing scrutiny over Telegram, which is widely used by millions of Russians. The warning underscores the potential security vulnerabilities associated with digital communication tools in military operations. As tensions continue, the focus on secure communication methods remains a priority for Russian authorities.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | German Chancellor Friedrich Merz Begins First Visit to China
data.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has commenced his inaugural visit to China, navigating a complex diplomatic landscape. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this trip amid ongoing geopolitical tensions. Merz's visit aims to strengthen bilateral relations and address key issues such as trade and climate change. The German leader's agenda includes meetings with Chinese officials to discuss economic cooperation and global challenges. This visit comes at a time when international relations are increasingly strained, requiring careful diplomacy to foster mutual understanding and collaboration.

data.title: Bitcoin Open Interest Falls 54% Since All-Time High
data.description:
According to a report shared by CryptoQuant, Bitcoin’s derivatives market has undergone a significant structural reset following its latest all-time high. Bitcoin Open Interest (OI), primarily tied to futures markets and representing the total dollar value of open contracts, peaked at approximately $47.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-open-interest-falls-54-since-all-time-high/

data.title: EU Fines X Platform €120 Million for Digital Services Act Violation
data.description:
The European Union has imposed a €120 million fine on X Platform, owned by Elon Musk, for allegedly violating the Digital Services Act, according to The Brussels Times. This penalty, issued by the European Commission in December last year, marks the first enforcement action under the EU's digital regulation law, which mandates large platforms to combat illegal content, misinformation, and abuse.

According to Jin10, X Platform has appealed the fine, arguing that the EU's investigation was flawed. In a statement released on its platform, X claimed the decision was based on an incomplete and superficial investigation, citing significant procedural errors, misinterpretation of Digital Services Act obligations, and systemic violations of defense and due process rights.

data.title: SBI Holdings Introduces Blockchain Bond with XRP Payments
data.description: SBI Holdings has unveiled a blockchain-based bond valued at 10 billion yen, specifically aimed at individual investors. According to NS3.AI, this bond provides immediate payouts in XRP tokens equivalent to the investment amount and includes additional XRP rewards as interest over a period of one to three years. This initiative represents a fusion of traditional finance and cryptocurrency, utilizing XRP for both principal and interest disbursements.

data.title: Japan SBI Launches On-Chain Bonds That Instantly Reward Investors With XRP
data.description:
Japan-based financial group SBI Holdings has launched on-chain bonds that grant holders an equivalent amount of XRP. SBI announced the initiative yesterday, marking what it describes as the first-ever on-chain Security Token (ST) bond issuance. Visit Website
source: https://thecryptobasic.com/2026/02/21/japan-sbi-launches-on-chain-bonds-that-instantly-reward-investors-with-xrp/
